market_overview.headline: Major indices hold Full Bull structure but show fragile EMA spreads amid Iran tensions.
market_overview.detail: SPY and QQQ both Full Bull but with tight EMA spreads (0.47-0.72%) creating bearish cross risk. 42% of stocks in Full Bull vs 26% in bear structures suggests selective strength.

items.name: Carvana Co.
items.crossover_detail: 8W just below 13W (0.13%) — bullish cross potential; 13W just below 21W (0.80%) — bullish cross potential
items.note: Strong momentum +13% above both 8W and 13W EMAs with dual bullish cross setup.

items.note: EDA software play rallying +7.8% above 8W with bullish cross potential.

items.note: Travel leader +6.3% above 8W showing strength despite bear structure.

items.name: Cheniere Energy, Inc.
items.note: LNG play benefiting from energy tensions with orderly low-volume pullback.

momentum_themes.headline: Tech Hardware Acceleration
momentum_themes.detail: Electronic technology showing explosive momentum with SNDK (+56%), LITE (+48%), and CIEN (+47%) leading. Storage and optical networking themes dominating as AI infrastructure build-out continues.

sector_analysis.headline: Energy Dominance vs Tech Bifurcation

strongest.note: Perfect bullish score with Iran tensions driving energy complex.
Industrial momentum with 94% bull participation rate.
AI hardware themes driving sector despite some laggards.

weakest.note: Software under pressure with 77% in bear structures.
Biotech weakness with 64% bearish despite pullback opportunities.
Banking sector mixed despite some reversal candidates emerging.

sector_analysis.detail: Clear rotation into energy and industrials while tech services and healthcare lag. Electronic tech bifurcated - hardware accelerating while software struggles.

items.text: SPY and QQQ showing tight EMA spreads with bearish cross risk - fragile bull structure
26% of stocks in bear structures vs 42% Full Bull - participation narrowing
Iran-US tensions creating volatility in energy complex and broader risk-off potential
BTC in bear structure while SPY/QQQ bullish - correlation divergence risk

news_drivers.headline: What Moved Markets
news_drivers.summary: Markets closed slightly lower on renewed US-Iran tensions and Hormuz Strait disruptions. Energy stocks rallied on supply concerns while broader market stayed cautious ahead of potential peace talks. Geopolitical uncertainty overshadowed corporate earnings themes.

items.text: Iran tensions and Hormuz shipping disruptions boosting energy sector
Oil prices rising on supply chain concerns affecting global trade
US-Iran peace talks uncertainty creating broad market caution
European markets sliding on ceasefire collapse concerns
China manufacturing costs rising due to shipping disruptions
